rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE FRANCES WALSH REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/23. (No earlier availability.) The net amount of cash distributable to each beneficiary appears to be incorrect, if there is $103,044.49 in cash on hand and a $20,000 reserve is to be withheld or if the $38,643.70 cash in the Portsmouth account as of 6/30/22 is added to the $103,044.49 in the Bank of America account as of 6/30/22. It appears that an amendment may need to be filed to correct the proposed distribution. If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. This has still not been addressed. Some of the services provided do not appear reasonable; this Court does not ordinarily allow fees for "research" regarding the Probate Code or local rules or for attorney time spent on secretarial work or work that could be done by a paralegal. Why was a proposed order not simply mailed to the Court (see cost charge on 11/30/21 and time entry on 1/31/22)? After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required for the Court to determine if the two accountings should be approved, where one accounting would have sufficed, beginning on the date the co-trustees were appointed, and if legal fees (and costs) requested and already paid without a Court order, are reasonable. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/22/2023. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The information required by LRSF 14.43(D)(2)-(4) must be provided. 2) The petition must be presented under Probate Code section 850 to request an order compelling "all non-party holders of property" to transfer the funds to the estate, and each non-party property holder must be served in the manner required by section 851(a)(2). See also section 856. 3) The petition requests the Court's retention of jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement under CCP section 664.6, but the underlying 850 petition was taken off calendar at the 10/26/2022 hearing without objections and is no longer pending. Do the parties to the settlement agreement qualify as "parties to pending litigation" within the meaning of section 664.6(a)? The settlement agreement does not contain a provision requiring any party to request the Court's retention of jurisdiction, nor was the Court presented with the request to retain jurisdiction before the 850 petition was taken off calendar. See Hagan Engineering, Inc. v. Mills (2003) 115 Cal.App.4th 1004; see also Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960. 4) The settlement agreement contains a confidentiality clause, but an unredacted copy of the agreement has already been filed and is publicly viewable.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALICE M. WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/11/23. (No earlier availability.) As noted previously, notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Furthermore, this amendment to the amended petition (which was actually second fully amended pleading, not just an amendment to the amended petition) was not filed until 2/14/23, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. In order for the Court to treat the previously paid attorney fees as extraordinary fees, time sheets or a fully detailed fee declaration must be filed. [CRC 7.702.] (The personal representative does not need to verify the attorney's verified fee declaration.) (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS APPOINTED.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and proof of service of notice of the hearing on 4/11/23 must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/30/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/23. (No earlier availability.) If Alma Castlebury was the decedent's mother's sister, Alma's daughter Betty Butterfield was the decedent's mother's niece, not cousin. Betty Butterfield was the decedent's cousin, making her daughter, Donna Bateman, the decedent's first cousin once removed. If Betty Butterfield died on 11/26/22, she survived the decedent. Who are Betty Butterfield's successors-in-interest? The personal representative of her estate, trustee of her trust, or heirs if she died intestate, must be identified and be given notice. Were Frances Powell Griffin and Alma Castlebury the only children of Laudie Powell and Amanda Thirston? If the petitioner does not know the identities of all of the decedent's heirs, a formal heir search must be conducted. Were there any "written instructions provided" to the proposed executor? If so, the document(s) must be filed and any beneficiaries named in the written instructions must be listed and must receive notice. The petition caption indicated that the will was lost Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner before 4/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERVYN WYNN MEMORIAL TRAVEL FUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Fourteenth Account And Report Of De La Salle Institute And Petition For Settlement Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/25/23. (No earlier availability.) Barely legible Exhibit B is just the Commonfund spreadsheet for the period 4/1/19 - 4/30/19 showing the adjusted cost basis as $104,130.80, but that is not an explanation of why the cost basis was changed from $217,382.82. Did the petitioner and attorney intend to request fees for the attorney? This was not in the petition caption or prayer or proposed order. If so, the petition must be amended. It appears that $9,105.35 in legal fees were already paid. Under what authority? Are all of the billing parties attorneys? If any is a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. If $53.40 charged on 4/30/22 was for copies of prior petitions and orders attached to this petition, that is not a reasonable cost. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/14/23. (An amendment or supplement should just stand on its own and not refer to tentative rulings or an examiner's notes; different or additional information, as the case may be, should just be set forth in the amendment or supplement.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CUDD, JR. TRUST, DATED JUNE 16, 1987
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/25/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Please file a copy of the statement immediately preceding the date of death. A beneficiary of a trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Who are all of the heirs of the settlors other than Susan Kim Stroman and Jeffery Allan Cudd? ("Beneficiary" is not a relationship, which should be the familial relationship or "stranger" if a person is not family.) Are Deborah Ann Cudd and Deborah Ann Murray the same person? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/14/23. (A supplement to a petition under Probate Code Section 850 also requires 30 days' notice with a copy of the pleading. [CRC 7.53(b).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Successor Trustee. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.] The Court will not make an order that was not prayed. In general, if a successor trustee brings a petition under an instrument that the successor trustee deems to be a valid trust, the Court will not make an order that the trust is valid.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HUGHES J. HEBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the holographic will dated July 14, 2001, should be admitted to Probate and if Andre Simon Hebert should be appointed as the administrator with will annexed with full IAEA authority and no bond. No new Proof of Holographic Instrument has been filed, signed by a disinterested party. Courtesy copies of two "declarations" filed on 2/16/23 and 2/17/23 by Barbara R. Simon and the petitioner, respectively, have not yet been received by this examiner as of 2/22/23. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
